query: SpaceX Starbase Pad 2 deluge tests Ship 40 Booster 20 static fire Flight 13

mako.raw:
{"query":"SpaceX Starbase Pad 2 deluge tests Ship 40 Booster 20 static fire Flight 13","model":"mako","response_time_ms":758,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://spaceflightnow.com/2026/07/16/live-coverage-spacex-to-deploy-first-starlink-v3-satellites-on-suborbital-starship-super-heavy-flight/","title":"Post-ignition anomaly causes abort of SpaceX’s Starship Flight 13 – Spaceflight Now","content":"24 hours ago — SpaceX experienced a last-second abort during the launch attempt of Starship Flight 13 on July 16, 2026.
The Starship Flight 13 mission will be the second launch of a third-generation Starship-Super Heavy launch vehicle and the second mission for the program this year.
When it flies, SpaceX will launch this mission using Booster 20 and the Ship 40 upper stage.
One of the biggest differences between Flight 13 and Flight 12, which launched in May, is that this time around, SpaceX will be deploying 20 production Starlink V3 satellites.
“Software testing between spacecrafts will help demonstrate that the commercial human landing system prototypes and Orion can meet at a precise time and location in space,” NASA said in a press release on Wednesday.
“When Orion docks with the Blue Moon test lander, the Orion spacecraft’s software will control the docked spacecraft.
Meanwhile, the SpaceX test article will control the docked spacecraft for the second portion of the mission.”
The Starlink 10-15 mission came a day after SpaceX launched a trio of spacecraft to study the effects of the Sun."},{"url":"https://www.space.com/space-exploration/launches-spacecraft/spacex-ignites-all-33-powerful-engines-on-starship-booster-test-ahead-of-flight-13-test-launch","title":"SpaceX ignites all 33 powerful engines on Starship booster ahead of Flight 13 launch | Space","content":"8 days ago — SpaceX conducts a static fire test with Ship 40, the upper-stage spacecraft slated SpaceX fires up all 6 of Starship's engines ahead of 13th ...Read more
SpaceX today (July 10) completed a brief static fire of the Starship Super Heavy booster tapped to launch the 13th test flight of the massive, mega-lift vehicle.
By early Friday, SpaceX began preparations leading up to the prelaunch engine test, including closing Boca Chica beach around 8 a.m.
EDT (1200 GMT) and transferring fuel to the pad's tank farm ahead of loading propellant onto the vehicle.
This is the second "Version 3" (V3) booster to reach the pad at Starbase for testing, and is equipped with 33 of SpaceX's upgraded Raptor 3 engines.
EDT (1500 GMT), and underwent a roughly 25-second burn simulating on the launch stand the duration and flight conditions for an actual launch.
The successful completion of Booster 20's static test fire paves the way for Starship's upcoming test launch, Flight 13.
Ship 40 and Booster 20, the Starship vehicles that will fly on Flight 13, will follow the same set of mission objectives to shake out the remainder of kinks in Starship V3.
As Ship's altitude decreases toward sea level, the vehicle performs a "flip and burn" maneuver that swings the whole stage upright and arrests its descent for a soft touchdown — eventually back at the Starbase pad on future launches, but once again in the Indian Ocean for Starship's upcoming mission.
Flight 13 will give SpaceX a chance to demonstrate that descent profile again using Ship 40, while also working through the booster and engine issues encountered during Flight 12."}]}

Coming soon: Flight 13, potentially as soon as NEXT WEEK!","score":0.8452414,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mgb-oYXAoY8","title":"Starship Flight 13 Timeline Taking Shape | B20 Passes First Cryo Test | Starship Update","content":"Starship Flight 13 Timeline Taking Shape | B20 Passes First Cryo Test | Starship Update

8 Jun 2026
Starship Flight 12 may be over, but SpaceX is already pushing hard toward Flight 13.

This week, Booster 20 rolled to Massey's for cryogenic proof testing and completed its first successful cryo test while Ship 40 continued preparations for static fire testing. We also examine what remains before both vehicles can be cleared for launch and discuss how Flight 13's timeline is shaping up.

Meanwhile, production continues across Starbase as Ship 41 and Booster 21 make progress toward Flight 14. We also check in on Raptor testing at McGregor, Gigabay construction in Texas and Florida, launch pad upgrades, and new clues about SpaceX's future plans including Starfall, orbital manufacturing, and asteroid mining concepts revealed through recent SpaceX materials.

🚀 Topics covered:
• Booster 20 cryogenic testing
• Ship 40 engine installation and testing plans
• Flight 13 launch timeline
• Ship 41 and Booster 21 progress
• Raptor 3 production and testing
• Starfactory updates
• Marmac 31 returns to Florida
• LC-39A and SLC-37 construction
• Pad 1 and Pad 2 upgrades
• Starfall and future SpaceX concepts
• Starlink V3 launch plans

What do you think the objectives for Flight 13 will be? Let us know in the comments.
